commit.message:
feat(ol-superset): sqlglot column validation for virtual datasets + SELECT * warnings

Uses sqlglot to parse virtual dataset SQL queries and validate that every
column referenced by a dependent chart actually appears in the query output.
Also warns when virtual datasets use SELECT * or table.* wildcards that
prevent static column analysis.

Changes:

asset_index.py:
- Add sqlglot + sqlglot.expressions imports
- Add VirtualColumnsResult(NamedTuple) with columns: set[str]|None and
  has_wildcard: bool fields
- Add extract_virtual_dataset_columns(sql) — parses outermost SELECT,
  returns exact lowercase column names; returns (None, has_wildcard=True)
  for SELECT */table.* and (None, has_wildcard=False) on parse failure
- DatasetAsset gains virtual_columns: set[str]|None and sql_has_wildcard: bool
- _load_datasets() calls extract_virtual_dataset_columns() for every virtual
  dataset and stores the result on the DatasetAsset

validate.py (section [4/4]):
- 4b: warn when dataset.sql_has_wildcard — surface it as an actionable
  message with the suggestion to replace with an explicit column list
- 4c: for virtual datasets, compare chart column refs against
  virtual_columns (case-insensitive lowercase); skip only when
  virtual_columns is None (wildcard or unparseable)
- 4c: for simple datasets, continue using columns|calculated_columns as before
- Distinguish message wording: 'SQL output' vs 'column list' per type

pyproject.toml:
- Add sqlglot>=20.0 to runtime dependencies

tests/test_asset_index.py (+9 tests, 37 total):
- Updated fixture: virtual dataset uses explicit SELECT columns; added
  separate wildcard dataset (uuid=pqr999, SELECT a.*)
- test_build_asset_index_virtual_dataset: assert virtual_columns parsed
- test_build_asset_index_wildcard_dataset: assert sql_has_wildcard=True
- 8 new extract_virtual_dataset_columns tests covering: simple SELECT,
  aliases, case normalisation, CTE outer SELECT, bare SELECT *, table.*,
  parse error, empty string

Results on real assets: 2 SELECT * warnings for Program_Enrollment_with_user
and marts__mitxonline_course_engagements_daily; section_title genuinely
missing from marts__combined_video_engagements_w_video_counts SQL output
now correctly surfaced.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: batch generic-assay-meta requests by profile suffix (#5430)

multiple studies:

  then map results back to individual profile IDs

commit.message:
fix(ol-superset): exclude Superset-calculated columns from dbt YAML checks

Superset datasets can define 'calculated columns' — columns with a
non-empty `expression` field that are computed by Superset at query time
from other columns (e.g. `substring(order_created_on, 1, 4)`). These are
not warehouse columns and will never appear in a dbt model YAML, so
flagging them as 'not documented in dbt model YAML' produces false-positive
warnings.

Changes:
- DatasetAsset gets a new `calculated_columns: set[str]` field populated
  by `_load_datasets()`. Columns with a non-empty `expression` go there;
  plain warehouse columns go into `columns` as before.
- Validation 4a (dataset columns vs dbt YAML) now only iterates
  `dataset.columns`, skipping `calculated_columns` entirely.
- Validation 4c (chart column refs vs dataset) now resolves against
  `dataset.columns | dataset.calculated_columns`, because charts may
  legitimately reference calculated columns by name.
- Tests updated: fixture gains a calculated column; two new assertions
  verify the plain/calculated split. Total: 28 tests, all pass.

Result: 69 → 59 warnings (10 fewer false positives).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add AC charger (smart charger) support

Add support for AcCharger device type (BLE record type 0x08), which
covers Blue Smart IP22/IP65, Phoenix Smart IP43, and other Victron
AC charger models.

New sensor keys: AC_CURRENT, OUTPUT_VOLTAGE_1-3, OUTPUT_CURRENT_1-3.
Existing keys reused: CHARGE_STATE, CHARGER_ERROR, TEMPERATURE.

Also adds snapshot tests for all 8 supported device types.

commit.message:
fix(ol-superset): skip chart column checks for virtual (SQL-defined) datasets

Virtual datasets are defined by a SQL query rather than a direct table
reference. Their YAML `columns` list is a cached Superset metadata
snapshot that may not enumerate every column the SQL produces, so checking
chart column refs against it generates false-positive warnings.

Changes:
- [3/4] print an info line showing how many virtual datasets are skipped
  and why, so operators can see they are accounted for
- [3/4] success message now says 'simple Trino dataset(s)' and counts only
  non-virtual datasets to give an accurate picture
- [4/4] 4c (chart → dataset column check) now skips virtual datasets;
  their column surface is validated via SQL table-ref checks in 4b instead
- [4a/4b] comments clarified to explain the virtual/simple distinction

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Forms: Create synced form when inserting form variations via block inserter (#47335)

* Forms: Create synced form when inserting form variations via block inserter

When users insert a form variation directly from the block inserter (e.g.,
"Contact Form"), WordPress creates the block with innerBlocks immediately,
bypassing the VariationPicker. This meant synced forms were not being created.

This adds a new hook `useCreateSyncedFormOnInsertion` that detects when a form
block is inserted with inner blocks but without a ref attribute, and
automatically creates a synced form when central form management is enabled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Forms: Use variationName attribute and wasBlockJustInserted for synced form creation

Simplify the useCreateSyncedFormOnInsertion hook by:
- Using wasBlockJustInserted selector to detect newly inserted blocks
- Using variationName attribute directly instead of deriving variation from inner blocks
- Removing complex heuristic-based variation detection logic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Forms: Address review feedback for synced form creation hook

- Add entity preloading before setting ref to prevent skeleton flash
- Replace unstable useEffect deps with empty array and ref guard
- Remove unnecessary registry.batch() wrapper around single dispatch
- Update getVariationTitle docblock to accurately describe return cases
- Replace flaky setTimeout in tests with deterministic microtask flushing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Forms: Simplify synced form creation hook for performance

- Short-circuit useSelect after creation attempt to stop unnecessary store queries
- Collapse guard clauses into single shouldCreate boolean
- Match variations by name instead of attributes.variationName
- Add variationName to variations missing it so name-based lookup works
- Simplify tests by removing redundant mock data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Forms: Fix TypeScript error for currentPostId type mismatch

getCurrentPostId() returns string | number, but createSyncedForm
expects number. Wrap with Number() to satisfy the type checker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use variationName in insertion logic

Only create a synced form on insertion if the block has a variationName: add a truthy attributes.variationName check to the shouldCreate condition and include it in the dependency array. Also fix the variation title lookup to compare against variations' attributes.variationName (not v.name) so the correct form title is selected.

* Prevent post-unmount updates in form insertion hook

Add cancellation handling and safer entity preload in useCreateSyncedFormOnInsertion: introduce a cancelled flag with a cleanup return to avoid setting attributes or showing notices after unmount, wrap getEntityRecord in try/catch as a best-effort preload, and bail early when cancelled. Update tests to cover preload failure (still sets ref), adjust variationName test data, ensure no synced form is created when variationName is missing, and add a test for unknown variationName fallback behavior.

* Forms: Add useEffect dependencies to fix synced form creation for variations

The empty dependency array meant the effect only ran on mount. If
shouldCreate was false initially (e.g. innerBlocks not yet populated),
the synced form would never be created. The hasAttemptedCreation ref
prevents double execution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Reset creation flag on cleanup and silence deps

Reset hasAttemptedCreation.current to false in the effect cleanup so the creation attempt flag is cleared when the component is unmounted. The effect's dependency array was replaced with an empty array and an eslint-disable comment was added to suppress react-hooks/exhaustive-deps; this makes the effect run only once on mount (intentional to avoid repeated creation attempts). Previously the effect listed shouldCreate, attributes, innerBlocks, currentPostId, setAttributes, createSuccessNotice, and createErrorNotice as dependencies.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ol-superset): add dbt dependency-chain validation to ol-superset validate

Extends `ol-superset validate` with an optional `--dbt-dir` / `-b` flag
that validates the full Dashboard → Chart → Dataset → dbt model dependency
chain before assets are promoted to production.

New modules:
- ol_superset/lib/dbt_registry.py — parses dbt model YAML schema files into
  a name-keyed registry; reads dbt_project.yml + profiles.yml to derive the
  expected Trino schema for each model layer (staging, marts, reporting, etc.)
- ol_superset/lib/asset_index.py — builds UUID-keyed lookups for all local
  Superset dataset, chart and dashboard YAML files; extracts plain column
  references from chart params (SQL-expression dicts are skipped)

Four validation checks added (in order):
  [1/4] Dashboard → chart UUID cross-references (ERROR if missing)
  [2/4] Chart → dataset UUID cross-references (ERROR if missing)
  [3/4] Dataset table_name / schema match in dbt registry (ERROR/WARNING)
  [4/4] Chart column refs exist in dataset column list (WARNING)

Column mismatches are warnings because dbt YAML may not document every column
that exists in the warehouse; UUID mismatches are hard errors.

Also adds 27 unit tests covering both new lib modules and updates README with
flag documentation and check table.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(versioning): add schedule version management with compare

* Add version-store.js — save/load/delete/rename/star versions in localStorage
* Add version-compare.js — cell-level diff and side-by-side comparison view
* Add version-ui.js — Versions tab panel with cards, actions, compare bar
* Add versioning.css — styling for version panel, cards, badges, compare
* Add 5th "Versions" tab in tab bar (enabled after first generation)
* Auto-save schedule snapshot after each successful generation
* Register versions tab in tabs.js TAB_CONFIG and DISPLAY_MAP
* Hook onVersionAutoSave() into generate.js post-render pipeline
* Add 43 unit tests for version-store and version-compare modules

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

@wordpress/ui: add Collapsible component (#76280)

* Add Collapsible component to @wordpress/ui

Thin wrapper around Base UI's Collapsible component with Root,
Trigger, and Panel subcomponents. No custom styles — this is a
pure pass-through of the Base UI API.

Made-with: Cursor

* Add Storybook stories for Collapsible

Covers default, default-open, disabled, and controlled variants.

Made-with: Cursor

* Add unit tests for Collapsible

Covers ref forwarding, uncontrolled/controlled open state,
disabled behavior, and className pass-through.

Made-with: Cursor

* Update CHANGELOG for Collapsible component

Made-with: Cursor

* Address review feedback

- Fix import ordering: external (@base-ui) before @wordpress
- Add render prop tests for Root, Trigger, and Panel

Made-with: Cursor

* Update CollapsibleCard to use internal Collapsible wrapper

Replace direct @base-ui/react/collapsible imports with the new
internal Collapsible component in all three CollapsibleCard files.

In header.tsx, derive the open state from the aria-expanded prop
instead of the Base UI state callback, since our wrapper's render
prop type uses a single-argument signature.

Made-with: Cursor

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comment explaining aria-expanded tradeoff in header render prop

The Collapsible wrapper's ComponentProps utility narrows the render
callback to a single-argument signature, so Base UI's `state` object
isn't available. Document why we read open state from `aria-expanded`.

Made-with: Cursor

* avoid unnecessary destructuring

* add hidden until found example

---------

Co-authored-by: ciampo <mciampini@git.wordpress.org>
Co-authored-by: mirka <0mirka00@git.wordpress.org>

commit.message:
refactor: eliminate circular import workarounds across codebase

Move inline imports to module-level in orchestrator.py, agent_runner.py,
beads_hierarchy.py, and beads_recovery.py. This removes 7+ inline imports
that were working around circular dependencies by:

- Moving imports from function bodies to module top-level
- Updating test mock patch targets to match new import locations

The modules no longer have actual circular dependencies, so these inline
imports were unnecessary legacy workarounds.

Closes: PokePoke-xrxm

commit.message:
Fix pyarrow schema mismatch and add row_hash merge key for edxorg dlt CSV/TSV loading (#1967)

* Initial plan

* Fix pyarrow schema mismatch in grades_persistentsubsectiongrade data loading

Add TABLE_DTYPE_OVERRIDES to force first_attempted column to VARCHAR for
grades_persistentsubsectiongrade, preventing ArrowInvalid errors when
concat_tables encounters inconsistent type inference across TSV files.

Co-authored-by: blarghmatey <479088+blarghmatey@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use all_varchar=True for general type consistency across all dlt CSV sources

Replace the per-table TABLE_DTYPE_OVERRIDES workaround with all_varchar=True
in the read_csv_duckdb call, which forces all columns to VARCHAR for every
table. This prevents pyarrow ArrowInvalid schema mismatch errors regardless
of which table or column has inconsistent type inference across TSV files.
Type casting is handled downstream by dbt models.

Co-authored-by: blarghmatey <479088+blarghmatey@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use row_hash as merge key for edxorg dlt iceberg loading

Switch write_disposition from "append" to "merge" and set primary_key to
"row_hash" so that dlt deduplicates records on load. All edxorg TSV exports
include a row_hash column, making it a reliable merge key for iceberg tables.

Co-authored-by: blarghmatey <479088+blarghmatey@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix quoting mismatch in edxorg_archive.py TSV write

Add quote_style="never" to sink_csv to match the quote_char=None used
when reading the source TSVs. Polars' default quoting wraps some values
in double-quotes; DuckDB then sees quoted vs. unquoted values for the
same column across files, which was a contributing factor to the
inconsistent type inference that caused pyarrow schema mismatch errors.

Co-authored-by: blarghmatey <479088+blarghmatey@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cross-course row_hash collision and improve comment style

- Use composite primary_key=["row_hash", "extracted_course_key"] to prevent
  record collisions across courses. The row_hash is computed in Polars
  with_columns on the original CSV columns only (pl.all() does not see
  the extracted_* columns added in the same call), so rows with identical
  source content from different courses would otherwise collide on row_hash
  alone, causing silent data loss during merge.
- Update comment block for all_varchar=True to use proper sentence case
  and punctuation per style guide.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: blarghmatey <479088+blarghmatey@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Tobias Macey <tmacey@boundlessnotions.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Use sup CLI's InteractiveOAuthAuth for token caching in ol-superset

Replace the custom PKCE OAuth implementation in superset_api.py with
preset_cli's InteractiveOAuthAuth, which is the same auth class used
by the sup CLI.

This means tokens are now cached in ~/.sup/tokens/<hostname>.json and
reused across invocations. The interactive browser flow is only
triggered when no valid cached token (or refresh token) exists,
eliminating constant re-authentication.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Contradiction detection symmetry
- Invalid confidence rejection

commit.message:
Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(superset/rls): add ol_data_analyst to course scope bypass filter

Education technologists assigned to ol_data_analyst need unrestricted
cross-course and cross-platform visibility in the Course Engagement
dashboard. The existing instructor_enrollment_scope_with_term_expiry
Regular filter restricted ol_instructor users to only their own
courses (causing only 2 of 4 platforms to appear in the Platform
native filter).

Rename admin_engineer_course_scope_bypass to
admin_engineer_analyst_course_scope_bypass and add ol_data_analyst to
its roles list. This Base filter ensures analysts bypass course-scope
RLS for all per-learner engagement and enrollment datasets, consistent
with the ol_data_analyst access pattern defined in the governance
framework.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
